template: You are an expert QA/QC engineer responsible for preparing a comprehensive, field-ready Quality Inspection Checklist for {{work_title}}, related to the {{discipline}}, to be used directly during site inspections by construction and quality personnel, in accordance with reference standards: {{ref_standards}}, project specifications, approved drawings, and considering all constraints, site conditions, and remarks provided in: {{remarks}}; you must generate a complete, structured, and highly practical Quality Inspection Checklist designed for real-time field use, ensuring clarity, traceability, and strict compliance with construction quality management systems. The checklist shall systematically break down {{work_title}} into logically sequenced inspection checkpoints aligned with the {{discipline}} workflow, covering all relevant stages such as material receipt and approval, pre-installation readiness, execution activities, in-process controls, final installation verification, dimensional and tolerance checks, testing coordination, punch list identification, and final acceptance verification. Each checklist item must be expressed as a clear, binary or measurable inspection statement (e.g., Yes/No, Compliant/Non-Compliant, Within Tolerance/Out of Tolerance), ensuring it is directly verifiable on site without ambiguity or interpretation. For each inspection item, define applicable reference requirements strictly derived from {{ref_standards}} and project specifications, acceptance criteria that are measurable, objective, and inspection-testable, and required verification evidence such as MIR, IR, checklists, test reports, calibration certificates, inspection photos, or as-built records. The checklist must ensure full lifecycle coverage of {{work_title}} including pre-activity readiness verification (materials, equipment, drawings, permits, manpower), execution quality controls (alignment, dimensions, workmanship, installation sequence, environmental conditions), testing and inspection requirements, interface checks with adjacent works, safety and protection compliance impacting quality, and final handover readiness including punch list clearance and documentation completeness. The structure shall be optimized for field usability, with concise, action-oriented inspection statements, logically grouped under execution phases, enabling rapid on-site verification by inspectors without requiring additional interpretation or narrative explanation. The final output must be strictly in tabular format only with columns: Stage, Inspection Item, Acceptance Criteria, Reference Standards, Verification Method/Evidence, QA/QC Role, and Remarks/Status, without any additional text, explanation, or narrative outside the table, ensuring the checklist is immediately deployable for site quality inspections and project quality control systems.
